english: pa źig (pa zhig) one having the nature or the colour of smoke Glr.; rta (rta) bcus (bcus) rgyug-pa (rgyug pa) ltá-bui (lta bu'i) sgra (sgra) a noise as if ten horses were galloping Glr.; ... ltá-bu (lta bu) mkás-pa (mkhas pa) źig (zhig) a man as wise as ... Dzl.; p'a-má (pha ma) ltá-bur (lta bur) gyúr-to (gyur to) he was (to him) like a father Dzl.; bai (ba'i) dzí-ma (rdzi ma) ltá-bu (lta bu) daṅ (dang) ldán-te (ldan te) having eyelashes like those of a cow Stg.; raṅ-la (rang la) mi-mkó-ba (mi mkho ba) bú-la (bu la) byiṅ-pa (bying pa) ltá-bu (lta bu) ma (ma) yin (yin) not as if she (the mother) would give her child only what she does not want herself Thgy.; źes-pa (zhes pa) ltá-buo (lta bu'o) is the usual expression for quoting a passage from an author, and always follows the quotation; kyod (khyod) ṅá-lta-bu (nga lta bu) min (min) you are not my equal, and also: you are not in my situation Mil.; di-lta-bu ('di lta bu), de-lta-bu (de lta bu), one like him, such a one as he; ci-lta-bu (ci lta bu) what sort of? saṅs-rgyas (sangs rgyas) śes (shes) byá-ba (bya ba) ci-lta-bu (ci lta bu) yin (yin) the so-called Buddha, what sort of being is he? what is meant by 'Buddha'? Dzl. ci-lta-bu-la (ci lta bu la) bskal-pa (bskal pa) źes (zhes) bgyi (bgyi) what sort of a thing is called 'Kalpa'? ji-lta-ba (ji lta ba) v. ji (ji); ji-lta-bu (ji lta bu) of what kind, as a rel. pron. Sometimes lta (lta) alone is used for lta-bu (lta bu): kyod-lta (khyod lta) your equal Mil.; so prob. also in the passage Dzl. 74V, 8, where yod-pa (yod pa) lta (lta) ci (ci) mtoṅ (mthong) would be = yod-pa (yod pa) ltá-bu (lta bu) gaṅ (gang) mtoṅ (mthong) (better than taking lta (lta) ci (ci) mtoṅ (mthong) for lta (lta) ci (ci) smos (smos) Schf.). In Dzl. 21V, 13, and 24V, 3 ltá-źig (lta zhig) is prob. to be altered into ltá-tog (lta tog), v. sub a, 2, above. — d. lta (lta) is sometimes a mere expletive, e.g. in da-lta (da lta) (v. da (da)), and after the conditional na (na) (Dzl. 72C, 1; 14C, b; 112, 16, 21L, b.).

I. vb., pf. bltas (bltas), fut. blta (blta), imp. ltos (ltos), blta (blta), resp. gzigs-pa (gzigs pa) (cf. ltos-pa (ltos pa)) 1. to look (as an act of the will, cf. mtoṅ-ba (mthong ba)), to view, often with mig (mig), or mig-gis (mig gis) (v. below); bltás-na (bltas na) mi (mi) mtoṅ (mthong) though you look (for it) you do not see it Mil.; *nán-tan (nan tan) źib-ča (zhib cha) ltos* (ltos) Ld. look at it accurately! *to (to) źig* (zhig) C. look (before you)! have your eyes open! *to (to) źig (zhig) nyoṅ (nyong) cig* (cig) C. attention! mind! be careful! ltá-bas (lta bas) čog (chog) mi (mi) śés (shes) I never can look enough at it; with nas (nas): to look from or through, sgo-śul-nas (sgo shul nas) (to peep) through the narrow opening of a door Tar.; bltá-na (blta na) sdúg-pa (sdug pa) pleasing when looked upon, charming to look at; also n. of the city of gods on the Riráb (ri rab) Stg., and of one of the seven golden mountains around the Riráb (ri rab) Glr.; ltá-ru (lta ru) soṅ (song) go there and look (at it)! *lta-la (lta la) toṅ* (tong) W. let me look (at it)! show it me! p'an-tsun-du (phan tshun du) ltá-ba (lta ba) to look around Dzl.; *čog-čóg-la* (cog cog la), or *ye-yoṅ-la* (ye yong la) col. id.; pyi (phyi) mig (mig), or pyir (phyir) (to look) back Dzl.; *pi (phi) mig (mig) log (log) lta-če* (lta che), or *jiṅ-pa (jing pa) gyúr-te (gyur te) ltá-če* (lta che) W. id.; to inspect, c.c.d., rarely c.a., frq. Glr., Dzl.; kyed (khyed) mi-nús-pa-la (mi nus pa la) bltás-na (bltas na) if one views, considers, your inability Dzl.; ṅas (ngas) ma (ma) bltas-na (bltas na) if I do not inspect it Glr.; *gháṅ-la (ghang la) tẹ (te) ruṅ* (rung) C. whatever one may fix his eyes upon = whatever it may be; to look after or into, to revise, to examine, to try, rtsa (rtsa) ltá-ba (lta ba) to feel a person's pulse Med.; p'án-nam (phan nam) blta (blta) I will see, if I can help Mil.; also: I will see, whether it has done good; su (su) če (che) blta (blta) let us see who is taller Mil.; e' (e) tsud (tshud) ltos (ltos) śig (shig) see, if you can put it through Glr.; rtiṅ-sor (rting sor) blta (blta) we shall see that afterwards Mil.; yaṅ-dag-par (yang dag par) ltá-ba (lta ba) to examine or search into minutely Mil.; *tsod (tshod) ltá-ba* (lta ba) in col. language is the expression most in use for to examine, to put to the proof, to test, to try, to sound etc. Lastly, as a mere act of the mind: to meditate, reflect, muse, ponder, investigate, du (du) dug (dug) blta (blta) let us see how many there are Mil.; lta (lta) rtog (rtog) byéd-pa (byed pa), or rtóṅ-ba (rtong ba) Mil. to investigate closely. Also in a mystic sense, v. sgóm-pa (sgom pa) I, 2. — 2. c.c.d. (or accus.) and termin., to look upon a thing as, śés-pa-la (shes pa la) zóg-tu (zog tu) to look upon knowledge as deceitful; dkon-mčóg (dkon mchog) ysum (gsum) mi (mi) bdén-par (bden par) ltá-ba (lta ba) to think the three treasures to be untrue, not real, = not to believe in them. — 3. c. dat. (rarely termin.): to have regard to, to pay attention to, to take notice of, and with a negative: to be indifferent to, not to care about, sróg-la (srog la) mi (mi) ltá-ba (lta ba) not to care about one's life (from heroism or desperation). — 4. to be situated or directed towards, mdo (mdo) ni (ni) núb-tu (nub tu) lta (lta) the lower part of the valley is situated towards the west. — 5. ṅas (ngas) bltás-pa (bltas pa) in my opinion:
